Live

SecureTest Application

A full-stack Next.js application designed to be deployed and tested by the SecDev platform. This app includes multiple pages, API endpoints, forms, and intentional security patterns that the SecDev testing pipeline can validate.

📊 6 Pages

Multiple routes for the test suite to discover and health-check via HTTP.

🔌 5 API Endpoints

REST API routes tested with GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, PATCH methods.

🔒 Security Headers

Some headers present, some intentionally missing for the security scan to flag.

⚡ Performance

Lightweight pages optimized for concurrent load testing benchmarks.

🧪 Form Inputs

Search forms and user inputs for XSS reflection testing.

♿ Accessibility

Semantic HTML with some intentional a11y issues for the vibetest agent.

Test Coverage Map

SecDev Agent What It Tests Expected Result

SecDev Agent What It Tests Expected Result
Test Suite HTTP 200 on all 6 page routes All Pass
API Tests 5 API endpoints × 5 HTTP methods Valid responses
Security Scan Headers, XSS, cookies, info disclosure Findings reported
Performance 20 requests per route, 10 concurrent Low latency
Vibetest Links, console errors, a11y, UI Some findings

export default function AboutPage() { return (

About SecureTest

SecureTest is a purpose-built Next.js application designed as a testing target for the SecDev autonomous security platform. It provides realistic pages, API endpoints, and intentional security patterns that the SecDev testing pipeline can validate.

Architecture

  • Framework: Next.js 14 (App Router)
  • Language: TypeScript
  • Pages: 6 routes (/, /dashboard, /users, /products, /settings, /about)
  • API: 5 endpoints (/api/health, /api/users, /api/products, /api/search, /api/config)
  • Data: In-memory mock data (no database required)

Security Testing Targets

  • Pass X-Content-Type-Options header set
  • Fail Missing Content-Security-Policy
  • Fail Missing Strict-Transport-Security
  • Warn Server version disclosed via X-Powered-By
  • Pass XSS payloads properly escaped

• Fail Missing Strict-Transport-Security
  • Warn Server version disclosed via X-Powered-By
  • Pass XSS payloads properly escaped

    • How to Use

    1. Deploy this repo via SecDev using the GitHub integration
    2. Wait for the sandbox to build and go live
    3. Run all test suites from the SecDev console Testing tab
    4. Review results — security findings, performance metrics, and route health
    5. Use the suggestions to improve your security posture
    ); }
